mandates: M1: Maintain platform uptime ≥ 99.97% monthly. M2: Zero tolerance for data breaches involving PII. M3: Full regulatory compliance in every operating jurisdiction. M4: All content properly rights-cleared before publication. M5: Financial reporting accuracy — zero material misstatements. M6: No unauthorized use of customer data. These mandates are encoded as hard constraints in the CORE Graph; agents cannot trade them off against objectives or goals.
mandate_enforcement: M1: Operational Twin monitors uptime; any degradation below 99.97% triggers automatic incident response + CISO escalation. M2: Security agents block any data egress not matching approved patterns; violation triggers immediate Legal Director + CISO escalation. M3: Legal & Compliance Governer Agent continuously monitors; novel regulatory situations escalate to Legal Director. M4: Rights and Licensing Subgraph consulted before any publication; uncertainty triggers rights hold + escalation. M5: Financial Stewardship agents run continuous reconciliation; discrepancies >$1K flag for CFO review. M6: Data Governance Agent enforces access controls; any anomalous access pattern triggers security exception.
goals: G1: Grow ad-tier subscribers to 80M globally (from 55M). Owner: Market & Ecosystem Governer Agent. Measured: Monthly subscriber count by tier. G2: Achieve $50B total revenue. Owner: Financial Governance Governer Agent. Measured: Monthly revenue rollup. G3: Launch NetStreamX Live with 48 live events. Owner: Enterprise Strategy Governer Agent. Measured: Event count + peak concurrent viewers. G4: Improve operating margin to 28%. Owner: Financial Governance Governer Agent. Measured: Quarterly P&L. G5: Reduce content delivery cost per stream-hour by 12%. Owner: Financial Governance + Operational Continuity. Measured: Infrastructure cost / total stream-hours.
goal_priorities: Priority stack: G4 (margin) > G2 (revenue) > G1 (ad-tier growth) > G3 (Live launches) > G5 (cost reduction). If ad-tier growth spend threatens margin targets, ADAM must constrain acquisition spending and generate an exception for the CFO. If Live event costs exceed allocation, defer events rather than exceed budget. These priority orderings are quarterly-configurable by the CEO and CFO.
planning_cadence: Annual goals set by board in December. Quarterly refinement: Weeks 11-12 of prior quarter. CEO + CFO update doctrine parameters (targets, budgets, priority orderings). Enterprise Strategy Governer Agent ingests updated doctrine and cascades constraints to all Domain Governors within minutes. Work-group agents receive updated execution parameters through the Orchestration Plane. Mid-year strategy review may trigger doctrine version change.
objectives_aspirational: O1: Become the most-loved entertainment brand globally (NPS > 50). O2: Establish NetStreamX as the definitive platform for non-English language storytelling. O3: Pioneer AI-personalized interactive storytelling. O4: Be recognized as the industry leader in creator-friendly business practices. O5: Achieve net-zero carbon in all operations by 2030.
deprioritization_signals: O1: Measured by quarterly NPS survey + social sentiment analysis. Deprioritize signal: NPS improvement requires >$500M annual investment with no revenue correlation. O2: Measured by non-English viewing hours as % of total + creator pipeline diversity metrics. O3: Measured by interactive title launches + engagement metrics. Deprioritize signal: User testing shows <5% engagement with interactive features after 3 quarters.
